recent_calls.summary: The user called Budget but was unable to clearly provide their service address ZIP code. The agent attempted to assist but the call ended abruptly after a short exchange.
The user, Mary, called Budget and indicated the call was about billing. The agent acknowledged and then transferred the call to another number for further assistance.

Mary called to schedule an appointment for her HVAC unit that is not heating. The agent collected her information, including her home warranty dispatch number, and confirmed a preferred appointment time for next Tuesday afternoon. Mary expressed interest in home maintenance programs and gave positive feedback about the call.
The user called Budget and requested to speak with customer service. The agent acknowledged the request and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.
The call was very brief and ended immediately after the agent's initial greeting and request for the user's ZIP code. No further interaction occurred.
The user called to check the status of a purchase order related to HVAC services. The agent asked for details about the order and the user's role, but the call ended abruptly before completion.

The user, Chasity Baker, called to check if Budget was still interested in partnering as they had not received the partnership packet back. The agent confirmed the user's contact details and assured that the message would be passed to the team for follow-up. The call ended with both parties confirming the information and a polite goodbye.
The user requested to speak with a live agent, and the initial agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was completed successfully without any issues.
The agent Sophie called Susan Crutcher to offer assistance, but the user questioned the reason for the call. The call ended shortly after without further discussion or resolution.
The user requested to speak with a live person, and the agent promptly transferred the call to another live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.

Esther from American Home Shield called Budget to follow up on a diagnosis for an AC unit at Cameron Young's address and inquired if it had been submitted for authorization. The agent confirmed the details and informed Esther that someone from Budget would contact her shortly. Esther declined information about maintenance programs and agreed to provide feedback.

Napoleon from American Home Shield called to reopen a completed work order for a cooling system check at 46 Juniper Drive for homeowner Talisa Love. The agent collected the homeowner's contact details and preferred appointment time, then confirmed the information and offered maintenance program details, which the user declined. The call ended with the user requesting to speak to a live agent, and the call was successfully transferred.

The user, Mark Pitts, called to schedule a plumbing service for a leak under his kitchen sink. The agent confirmed the details and booked an appointment for Thursday at noon. The user was satisfied with the service and acknowledged the agent was an AI assistant.

The user called to order a service and requested to speak directly with a human agent named Nicole instead of the AI. The agent acknowledged the request and successfully transferred the call to a live agent.
The user requested to speak with a customer service associate, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.
The user requested to speak with a live agent, and the initial agent transferred the call to another live agent successfully. The call lasted 13 seconds and ended with a call transfer.

Melissa Bax called to request immediate heating and cooling service because her system was not turning on. The agent confirmed the correct service address and booked an appointment for Thursday, April 2, 2026, at 4:00 PM. Melissa provided feedback rating the experience as 4 out of 5 and suggested improvements for the AI assistant.

Philip called to report an emergency issue with his cooling system not working. The agent booked a service appointment for Thursday, April 2, 2026, at 10:00 a.m. Philip declined to provide feedback and the call ended successfully.

Philip called to check on a service request and confirm if an appointment was scheduled. The agent could not find an appointment in the system and offered to transfer Philip to a live representative, which he accepted. The call was then successfully transferred to another agent.
The user returned a call but was unable to hear the previous caller. The agent attempted to assist by transferring the call to a live agent for further help.

April from American Home Shield called to provide a dispatch number and requested verification if a gas furnace needs replacement. The agent confirmed the details and the request to call the customer today for verification. The call ended with the user thanking the agent and no further requests.

Madeline called to schedule an AC check appointment through her HSA home warranty. The agent confirmed details including the dispatch number, service address, and preferred days for the appointment, and Madeline declined additional maintenance program information. The call ended positively with Madeline providing excellent feedback on the service.

Andrew Landress, a former HVAC technician, called to inquire about notary availability for notarizing his work hours for his Journeyman license. The agent collected his contact information and assured him that the team would follow up with him. The call ended with confirmation of the request and contact details.
The user called to return a call from the previous day. The agent acknowledged the request and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

Ingrid called Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing about having no hot water and a pilot light that is completely out. The agent confirmed the urgent heating service request for the same day and noted the service address and phone number. Ingrid declined information about maintenance programs and feedback requests, and the agent assured that a technician would contact her shortly.

The user called to schedule their next appointment related to an American Home Shield home warranty but did not specify the type of service needed despite the agent's requests. The call ended abruptly after the user declined to provide further details.

The user called to respond to a previous message about rescheduling their appointment to an earlier time. The agent acknowledged the request and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

The user called to inquire about getting a service appointment for their AC unit due to metal shavings. The agent clarified the urgency and type of service needed, but the user expressed dissatisfaction with the interaction and ended the call.

The user called to inquire about service availability in Florissant, Missouri, and reported an issue with their garbage disposal causing clogs. The agent confirmed service availability, collected the user's details, and noted the user's preference for the soonest available appointment. The user requested a service appointment and was informed about available time slots.
The agent mistakenly addressed the user as Alan, but the user corrected her. The user requested to speak to a person, and the agent transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

The user called to request cooling system service as their unit was not cooling and freezing up outside. Initially, the user preferred a same-day service, but the earliest available slots were on Thursday. After explaining the situation, the user upgraded the request to emergency service due to the heat, and the agent flagged it for immediate attention with the on-call team.

The user called to request an update on their AC work order after a technician visited yesterday. The agent confirmed the work order details, updated the callback number and service address, and assured the user that the team would follow up as soon as possible, ideally the next business day. The user declined to provide an email and had no additional instructions.

The user, Stacie Witney, called to schedule a same-day service appointment for her air conditioner, which is not cooling and freezing up outside. The agent confirmed her details and dispatch number, noted the service request, and informed her that the team would reach out to confirm availability. The call ended with the user declining to provide feedback at that moment.

Nina called to report that her brand new air conditioner is still not cooling despite two service visits and needs immediate attention. She requested a supervisor to be sent out and provided her callback number. The agent documented the issue and informed Nina that the team will call her back on the next business day since it was after-hours.

Alan called to schedule a plumbing service appointment for a valve issue on top of his water heater. The agent confirmed the details and scheduled the appointment for Tuesday morning, March 31st, at 8:00 AM. Alan declined to provide feedback and the call ended amicably.

Tracey called to report a busted pipe at her address requiring immediate attention. The agent documented the issue and informed her that the team would call back first thing the next business day since it was after-hours. Tracey requested a callback and had no further questions.

Tracey called to report water pouring into her kitchen and urgently requested to speak with an agent. The after-hours assistant, Evie, informed her that a live agent was not available but documented the issue as urgent and assured a callback on the next business day. Tracey confirmed her contact details and expressed the need for a callback, which was arranged.

Michayla called to report an emergency sewage backup at 4419 McMasters Avenue, Hannibal, Missouri. The agent confirmed the details and assured that the after-hours team will review the request and call her as soon as possible on the next business day. The emergency notification workflow was initiated during the call.

The user reported a gas leak and a valve issue on the hot water heater that required replacement. The gas company shut off the gas, and the agent confirmed the situation and flagged it as urgent for follow-up on the next business day. The user was informed that no immediate service could be provided after hours and decided to find someone else for immediate help.

The user, Marc, requested that Nicole call his office during regular business hours to schedule a home warranty preventive maintenance visit for his HVAC system. The agent confirmed the details, including the service address and contact number, and noted the request. The user declined to provide additional information or concerns, and the agent mentioned maintenance programs but did not receive a definitive response.

The user called to report an emergency water leak in the sink at their residence in Walden Spring, Missouri. The agent confirmed the details, including the address, contact information, and emergency status, and assured the user that the team would reach out the next business day. The user preferred to be contacted by phone for follow-up.

Shoshana Israel called to follow up on an AC service appointment arranged through American Home Shield. The agent confirmed her details, corrected the dispatch number, and submitted the service request for cooling system repair. Shoshana rated the AI assistant positively and provided feedback that it was easy to talk to.

Susan called to schedule a heating service appointment because her fan runs but doesn't blow heat. The agent confirmed the details and noted Susan's flexible availability for the appointment. Susan provided feedback, rating the AI assistant a 4 and expressing a preference for speaking with a real person.

The user called to request water heater service at their home in Saint Peters, Missouri. The agent collected the user's details, confirmed the service address, and noted the user's flexible availability for a service appointment. The user requested a callback to discuss service call pricing and confirm the appointment.

April from American Home Shield called to provide a dispatch number for a gas furnace replacement and confirmed that the customer will accept out-of-pocket costs. The agent confirmed the service address and email, and noted the request to have the customer called in the morning for further assistance and details. The user requested that all necessary details for the modification and upgrade be included when the team contacts the customer.

Stephanie called to schedule a routine HVAC maintenance service for the summer. She provided her availability as Monday through Friday, 8 AM to 5 PM, and confirmed her contact details and service address. The agent noted all details and confirmed the callback number, completing the scheduling process.

The user reported that their air conditioner is not working after a recent service visit, with no air blowing and water dripping from the water tank. The agent collected the user's details and assured that the team would be notified to address the issue. The call ended with the user satisfied and no further questions.
The user called and requested to speak to a South salesperson. The agent promptly transferred the call to the appropriate live agent. The call ended with a successful transfer.
